back_translation: After these events, there was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to the city of Jerusalem.
In the city of Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate there is a pool, which in the Hebrew language is called Bethsatha, with five porches surrounding it.
In those porches there was a great number of sick people lying — both blind people, lame people, and people with atrophied limbs.
There was a man there who had been ill for thirty-eight years.
When Jesus looked and saw that man lying there and knew that he had been ill for a long time, He said to him: 'Do you want to be made well?'
The sick man replied to Him: 'Sir, I have no one to help me down into the pool when the water stirs; while I am going down, someone else always goes down before me.'
Jesus said to him: 'Get up, take your mat and walk away.'
Immediately the man was made well, took his mat and walked. But that day was a Sabbath day.
The Jewish leaders therefore spoke to the man who had been healed: 'This is a Sabbath day; for you to carry your mat like this is contrary to the law.'
He answered them: 'The one who made me well, that one himself told me: "Take your mat and walk away."'
They therefore asked him: 'Who is the person who told you, "Take it up and walk"?'
The man who had been healed did not know who that one was, because Jesus had withdrawn Himself away, since there was a crowd in that place.
After this event, Jesus found him at the Temple and said to him: 'Look, now you have been made well; do not go back to sinning again, lest something worse happen to you.'
The man went away and told the Jewish leaders that the one who made him well was Jesus.
Because of this the Jewish leaders began to persecute Jesus, because He was doing these things on the Sabbath.
But Jesus answered them: 'My Father is still working until now, and I also am working.'
Because of this the Jewish leaders sought all the more to kill Him — not only because He broke the Sabbath, but also because He called God His own Father, which was making Himself equal to God.

Jesus then answered them: 'Amen, amen, I tell you, the Son cannot do anything on His own, except as He sees the Father doing; for whatever the Father does, the Son also does the same things in like manner.'
For the Father loves the Son and shows the Son everything He does, and will show the Son works greater than these, so that you will marvel.
For just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, so the Son gives life to whoever He wishes.
For the Father judges no one, but has committed all judgment to the Son.
So that all may give honor to the Son just as they give honor to the Father; whoever does not give honor to the Son does not give honor to the Father who sent the Son.
Amen, amen, I tell you, whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life, and will not be judged, but has passed from death into life.
Amen, amen, I tell you, that hour is coming, and now it has arrived, when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear will live.
For just as the Father has life in Himself, so He has given to the Son to have life in Himself.
And He has given to the Son the authority to execute judgment, because He is the Son of Man.
Do not be amazed at this; for the hour is coming, when all who are in graves will hear His voice.
And they will come out — those who did good will go out into the resurrection of life, those who did evil into the resurrection of judgment.
I cannot do anything on my own; I judge according to what I hear, and my judgment is just, because I do not seek my own will, but the will of the one who sent me.
If I bear witness for myself, my testimony is not legally valid.
There is another who bears witness about me, and I know that the testimony that one bears about me is valid.
You sent people to John, and he bore witness to the truth.
But I do not receive testimony from a person; I say these things so that you may receive salvation.
John was a lamp that was burning and shining; you also were willing to rejoice in his light for a short time.
But I have testimony greater than that of John, because the works that the Father gave me to complete — these works themselves which I am doing — bear witness about me, that the Father has sent me.
And the Father who sent me, He Himself has borne witness about me; you have never heard His voice at any time, and have not seen His form either.
And His word does not abide in you, because you do not believe in the one whom He sent.
You search the Scriptures, because you think that there is eternal life in those Scriptures; and those very Scriptures bear witness about me.
But you refuse to come to me so that you may have life.
I do not receive honor from people.
But I know you — you do not have love for God in yourselves.
I have come in the name of my Father, and you do not welcome me; if another comes in his own name, you will welcome that one.
How are you able to believe, when you receive honor from one another, but do not seek the honor that comes from God who is the only one?
Do not think that I will accuse you before the Father; the one who accuses you already exists — Moses, in whom you have placed your hope.
For if you had believed Moses, you would have believed me, because Moses wrote about me.
But if you do not believe in what he wrote, how will you believe in my words?
